revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある磨...」
revision.text:
'''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある[[磨崖仏]]およびその周囲にある[[横穴墓]]群の総称である。
== 概要 == 和田大仏は、[[須賀川]]市内からの[[福島空港]]アクセス道路（[[国道118号]]）の[[阿武隈川]]大仏大橋付近の丘の南斜面の岩壁に彫られた磨崖仏である。[[阿弥陀如来]]、あるいは[[大日如来]]の像と思われ、高さはおよそ3.6m<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk 和田大仏及び横穴墓群|うつくしま電子辞典]</ref>。岩壁のまわりには、[[古墳時代]]の終わりごろにつくられた[[横穴墓]]が20基ほどある。
伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同]]3年）[[弘法大師]]が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。
かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
== 周辺 == * [[須賀川牡丹園]] * [[乙字ケ滝]] * [[福島空港公園]]緑のスポーツエリア
== 交通アクセス == * [[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]から車で15分 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[水郡線]][[川東駅 (福島県)|川東駅]]から徒歩10分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 須賀川観光協会ブログ]
{{coord|37|16|10.5|N|140|23|54|E|region:JP-07|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わたたいふつおよひよこあなほせきくん}} [[Category:日本の磨崖仏]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]]

'''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある[[磨崖仏]]およびその周囲にある[[横穴墓]]群の総称である。
== 概要 == 和田大仏は、[[須賀川]]市内からの[[福島空港]]アクセス道路（[[福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]]）の[[阿武隈川]]大仏大橋付近の丘の南斜面の岩壁に彫られた磨崖仏である。[[阿弥陀如来]]、あるいは[[大日如来]]の像と思われ、高さはおよそ3.6m<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk 和田大仏及び横穴墓群|うつくしま電子辞典]</ref>。岩壁のまわりには、[[古墳時代]]の終わりごろにつくられた[[横穴墓]]が20基ほどある。
伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同]]3年）[[弘法大師]]が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。
かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
== 周辺 == * [[須賀川牡丹園]] * [[乙字ケ滝]] * [[福島空港公園]]緑のスポーツエリア
== 交通アクセス == * [[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]から車で15分 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[水郡線]][[川東駅 (福島県)|川東駅]]から徒歩10分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 須賀川観光協会ブログ]

伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同]]3年）[[弘法大師]]が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。
かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。

{{Location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=8|relief=1|caption={{Center|和田大仏及び横穴墓群の位置}}}} '''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある[[磨崖仏]]およびその周囲にある[[横穴墓]]群の総称である。
== 概要 == 和田大仏は、[[須賀川]]市内からの[[福島空港]]アクセス道路（[[福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]]）の[[阿武隈川]]大仏大橋付近の丘の南斜面の岩壁に彫られた磨崖仏である。[[阿弥陀如来]]、あるいは[[大日如来]]の像と思われ、高さはおよそ3.6m<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk 和田大仏及び横穴墓群|うつくしま電子辞典]</ref>。岩壁のまわりには、[[古墳時代]]の終わりごろにつくられた[[横穴墓]]が20基ほどある。
伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年）[[空海]]（弘法大師）が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。
かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。
== 周辺 == * [[須賀川牡丹園]] * [[乙字ケ滝]] * [[福島空港公園]]緑のスポーツエリア
== 交通アクセス == * [[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]から車で15分 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[水郡線]][[川東駅 (福島県)|川東駅]]から徒歩10分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 須賀川観光協会ブログ]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

revision.text:
[[ファイル:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|画像中央が和田大仏でその両脇に横穴墓がある]] '''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある[[磨崖仏]]およびその周囲にある[[横穴墓]]群の総称である。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|乳部が削り取られている]] 和田大仏は、[[須賀川]]市内からの[[福島空港]]アクセス道路（[[福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]]）の[[阿武隈川]]大仏大橋付近の丘の南斜面の岩壁に彫られた高さおよそ3.6mの磨崖仏である。[[阿弥陀如来]]あるいは[[大日如来]]の像と思われ<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk 和田大仏及び横穴墓群|うつくしま電子辞典]</ref>、 伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年）[[空海]]（弘法大師）が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。 かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
まわりの岩壁には、[[古墳時代]]の終わりごろにつくられた、当時阿武隈川流域を中心に支配していた豪族の墓跡と考えられている[[横穴墓]]が20基ほどある。
[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。
== 周辺 == * [[須賀川牡丹園]] * [[乙字ケ滝]] * [[福島空港公園]]緑のスポーツエリア
== 交通アクセス == * [[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]から車で15分 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[水郡線]][[川東駅 (福島県)|川東駅]]から徒歩10分
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 須賀川観光協会ブログ]

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombsfecture) - National Historic Site == * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}[[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
