[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|ja|横穴墓}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster]] to [[Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster]]: Text replacement - "Yokoguchi" to "Cave Tomb"
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs

=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Yokoana Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
