[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}

=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombsfecture) - National Historic Site == * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site

National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}}

=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]
{{Coord|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP-07|display=title}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombsfecture) - National Historic Site == * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site

Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft|12=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=draft|12=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|12=draft|12=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=draft|12=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|12=draft|12=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft|12=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft|12=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=draft|12=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=draft|12=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=draft|12=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群 up to rev 80890051
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|画像中央が和田大仏でその両脇に横穴墓がある]] '''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある[[磨崖仏]]およびその周囲にある[[横穴墓]]群の総称である。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|乳部が削り取られている]] 和田大仏は、[[須賀川市|須賀川]]市内からの[[福島空港]]アクセス道路（[[福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]]）の[[阿武隈川]]大仏大橋付近の丘の南斜面の岩壁に彫られた高さおよそ3.6mの磨崖仏である。[[阿弥陀如来]]あるいは[[大日如来]]の像と思われ<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk 和田大仏及び横穴墓群|うつくしま電子辞典]</ref>、 伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年）[[空海]]（弘法大師）が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。 かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
まわりの岩壁には、[[古墳時代]]の終わりごろにつくられた、当時阿武隈川流域を中心に支配していた豪族の墓跡と考えられている[[横穴墓]]が20基ほどある。
[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。
== 周辺 == * [[須賀川牡丹園]] * [[乙字ケ滝]] * [[福島空港公園]]緑のスポーツエリア
== 交通アクセス == * [[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]から車で15分 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[水郡線]][[川東駅 (福島県)|川東駅]]から徒歩10分

{{DEFAULTSORT:わたたいふつおよひよこあなほせきくん}} [[Category:日本の磨崖仏]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] to [[Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|画像中央が和田大仏でその両脇に横穴墓がある]] '''和田大仏及び横穴墓群'''（わだだいぶつおよびよこあなぼぐん）は、[[福島県]][[須賀川市]]和田字大仏にある[[磨崖仏]]およびその周囲にある[[横穴墓]]群の総称である。
== 概要 == [[ファイル:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|乳部が削り取られている]] 和田大仏は、[[須賀川市|須賀川]]市内からの[[福島空港]]アクセス道路（[[福島県道63号古殿須賀川線]]）の[[阿武隈川]]大仏大橋付近の丘の南斜面の岩壁に彫られた高さおよそ3.6mの磨崖仏である。[[阿弥陀如来]]あるいは[[大日如来]]の像と思われ<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk 和田大仏及び横穴墓群|うつくしま電子辞典]</ref>、 伝説では、[[808年]]（[[大同 (日本)|大同]]3年）[[空海]]（弘法大師）が 諸国行脚の際に和田大仏を彫ったと伝えられている<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm 市指定文化財等/須賀川市公式ウェブサイト]</ref>。 かつては乳不足の婦女子は大仏の乳部を削って粉を煮立てて飲むと乳がでるようになるという[[民間信仰]]があったため、乳のあたりが削られている。
まわりの岩壁には、[[古墳時代]]の終わりごろにつくられた、当時阿武隈川流域を中心に支配していた豪族の墓跡と考えられている[[横穴墓]]が20基ほどある。
[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）、須賀川市指定史跡に指定された。
== 周辺 == * [[須賀川牡丹園]] * [[乙字ケ滝]] * [[福島空港公園]]緑のスポーツエリア
== 交通アクセス == * [[東北自動車道]][[須賀川インターチェンジ|須賀川IC]]から車で15分 * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[水郡線]][[川東駅 (福島県)|川東駅]]から徒歩10分
