{{DEFAULTSORT:わたたいふつおよひよこあなほせきくん}} [[Category:日本の磨崖仏]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abukuma River|qq=}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport|qq=}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitabha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitabha|qq=}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|qq=}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Peony Garden|qq=}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Expressway|qq=}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawato Station (Fukushima)|qq=}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigun Line|qq=}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abukuma River|qq=}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport|qq=}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitabha|qq=}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|qq=}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abukuma River|qq=}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport|qq=}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitabha|qq=}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|qq=}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Peony Garden|qq=}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Expressway|qq=}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawato Station (Fukushima)|qq=}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigun Line|qq=}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abukuma River|qq=}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport|qq=}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitabha|qq=}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|qq=}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abukuma River|qq=}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport|qq=}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitabha|qq=}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|qq=}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Sukagawa Peony Garden|ja|須賀川牡丹園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Peony Garden|qq=}} * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, {{ill|Tohoku Expressway|ja|東北自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tohoku Expressway|qq=}} * 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kawato Station (Fukushima)|ja|川東駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawato Station (Fukushima)|qq=}}, {{ill|Suigun Line|ja|水郡線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suigun Line|qq=}}, {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, {{ill|Sukagawa, Fukushima|ja|須賀川市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the {{ill|Abukuma River|ja|阿武隈川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abukuma River|qq=}}, along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting {{ill|Fukushima Airport|ja|福島空港|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport|qq=}} to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either {{ill|Amitābha|ja|阿弥陀如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amitabha|qq=}} or {{ill|Vairocana|ja|大日如来|1=Vairocana|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|2=ja|3=大日如来|4=zh|5=毗卢遮那佛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|13=User:Immanuelle/Vairocana|qq=}}.<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}}, also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>

In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=}}

[[sn:ada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster]]
<!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:日本の磨崖仏]] [[Category:福島県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:須賀川市の歴史]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Sukagawa, Fukushima
revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://sukagawa-kankoukyoukai.jp/blog/?p=3183 Sukagawa Tourism Association Blog]

[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>

In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}

revision.text:
[[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}
