revision.comment: Text replacement - "Wikidata entry [[d:" to "Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

<!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Japanese Magaibutsu]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sukagawa]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

<!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Japanese Magaibutsu]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sukagawa]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

<!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Japanese Magaibutsu]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sukagawa]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

{{wikidata link|Q28689731}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

{{wikidata link|Q28689731}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|qq=|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|qq=|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|qq=|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|qq=|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|qq=|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
== Nearby Sites == * [[:en:Sukagawa Peony Garden|Sukagawa Peony Garden]] * {{ill|Otazuna Falls|ja|乙字ケ滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otazuna Falls|1=Otsuji Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369924}} * {{ill|Fukushima Airport Park|ja|福島空港公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Airport Park|1=Fukushima Airport Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593704}} Green Sports Area
== Access == * 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Sukagawa Interchange|ja|須賀川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa Interchange|1=Sukagawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664791}}, [[:en:Tōhoku Expressway|Tohoku Expressway]] * 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kawahigashi Station|Kawato Station (Fukushima)]], [[:en:Suigun Line|Suigun Line]], {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/East Japan Railway Company|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q28689731]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Wada no Daibutsu and Yokoanabo at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The central figure is Wada Daibutsu, flanked by horizontal cave tombs.]] '''Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs''' (和田大仏及び横穴墓群) is a collective name for a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} and the surrounding {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Wada Daibutsu, Wada, [[:en:Sukagawa, Fukushima|Sukagawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview == [[File:Wada no Daibutsu at Sukagawa Fukushima.jpg|thumb|The breast area has been scraped away.]] Wada Daibutsu is a {{ill|Magaibutsu|ja|磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magaibutsu|1=magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11239248}} carved into the rock wall on the southern slope of a hill near the Daibutsu Bridge of the [[:en:Abukuma River|Abukuma River]], along the {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|ja|福島県道63号古殿須賀川線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefectural Route 63|1=Fukushima Prefectural Road Route 63|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11593669}}, a road connecting [[:en:Fukushima Airport|Fukushima Airport]] to Sukagawa City. The statue stands approximately 3.6 meters tall and is believed to depict either [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] or [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]].<ref name="utsukushima_dictionary">[http://www.gimu.fks.ed.jp/shidou/jiten/cgi-bin/index.cgi?sheet=detail&name=%A4%EF%A4%C0%A4%C0%A4%A4%A4%D6%A4%C4%A4%AA%A4%E8%A4%D3%A4%E8%A4%B3%A4%A2%A4%CA%A4%DC%A4%B0%A4%F3&area=%BF%DC%B2%EC%C0%EE%BB%D4&hen=bk Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs | Utsukushima Digital Dictionary]</ref>
According to legend, [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], also known as Kōbō Daishi, carved Wada Daibutsu during his travels in 808 (Daido 3).<ref name="ssukagawa_city">[http://www.city.sukagawa.fukushima.jp/2981.htm Sukagawa City Designated Cultural Properties | Sukagawa City Official Website]</ref>
In the past, there was a folk belief that women with insufficient breast milk could scrape powder from the chest area of the statue, boil it, and drink it to stimulate milk production, which has led to the erosion of the statue's breast area.
Around the rock wall, there are about 20 {{ill|Yokoana tombs|lt=Cave tombs|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}, believed to be the burial sites of powerful local clans that ruled along the Abukuma River during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
In 1969 (Showa 44), Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tombs were designated as a {{ill|Municipal historic site|ja|市指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Municipal historic site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Sukagawa City.
